summary: PR 226 reorganized the monolithic mp assembly file by splitting `asm/melee/mp/code_80041C78.s` into source-named mp assembly units: `mpcoll.s`, `mplib.s`, and `mpisland.s`. The build list in `obj_files.mk` was updated to compile those three objects instead of the old single `code_80041C78.o`. There were no PR body details or review comments in the slice, so the rationale is inferred from the title, file movements, object list update, and embedded source filename strings such as `mpcoll.c`, `mplib.c`, and `mpisland.c`.

key_files.role: New assembly file containing the large mpcoll portion split out from the old mp blob; diff shows `.section .text # 0x80041C78 - 0x8005B4B4` and data strings including `mpcoll.c`.
New assembly file containing the mpisland portion beginning at `func_8005A6F8`; diff shows source filename data string `mpisland.c`.
Renamed from `asm/melee/mp/code_80041C78.s` and reduced after moving mpcoll/mpisland blocks out; retained mplib-related assembly/data including `mplib.c`.
Build object list changed from `asm/melee/mp/code_80041C78.o` to `mpcoll.o`, `mplib.o`, and `mpisland.o`.

what_changed: Added `asm/melee/mp/mpcoll.s` with 12964 lines of assembly/data formerly residing in the monolithic mp assembly file.
Added `asm/melee/mp/mpisland.s` with 1096 lines of assembly/data for the island-related mp code.
Renamed `asm/melee/mp/code_80041C78.s` to `asm/melee/mp/mplib.s` and removed the portions now represented by `mpcoll.s` and `mpisland.s`.
Updated `obj_files.mk` so the mp object list compiles `mpcoll.o`, `mplib.o`, and `mpisland.o` instead of the single `code_80041C78.o`.

Preserved raw assembly labels and function names such as `func_80041C78`, `func_80041C8C`, `func_8004D164`, and `func_8005A6F8`; this PR was organizational rather than a decompilation-to-C conversion.
smart_moves: Used embedded source filename strings as split-boundary evidence: `mpcoll.c`, `mplib.c`, and `mpisland.c` appear in the assembly/data, supporting the new file names.
Kept the object order explicit in `obj_files.mk` after splitting, which is important for preserving link order and matching addresses in a decompilation project.
Created source-named asm units instead of keeping an address-named blob (`code_80041C78.s`), making later C migration and ownership of symbols easier to search and review.
Limited the PR to file reorganization and build-list changes; no evidence suggests behavior or instruction content was intentionally changed beyond relocation between asm files.
decomp_lessons: When a large address-named asm blob contains multiple original source files, split it along evidence-backed boundaries before deeper matching work.
Source filename strings in `.data`/`.rodata` are useful anchors for naming assembly files and deciding where file boundaries likely are.
After splitting asm, update the object list in the same PR and preserve object order to avoid accidental link-order or address changes.
A file split can be valuable decomp scaffolding even when all symbols remain as `func_`/`lbl_` placeholders; it improves searchability and prepares smaller units for future C ports.

For future reviewers, low-comment PRs like this should be validated by checking object order, section placement, and that moved assembly/data still corresponds to the expected source filename strings.
naming_conventions: Replaced address-based file naming (`code_80041C78.s`) with subsystem/source-style names: `mpcoll.s`, `mplib.s`, and `mpisland.s`.
Kept unresolved function names in the established decomp placeholder format, e.g. `func_80041C78`, `func_8005A6F8`.
Kept unresolved data labels in the established address-label format, e.g. `lbl_803BD270`, `lbl_804D64A0`, `lbl_80458E88`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split the mp assembly at probable original translation-unit boundaries while leaving raw instructions intact.
Retained `.include "macros.inc"` and section declarations in the new asm files.
Used data strings such as `.asciz "mpcoll.c"`, `.asciz "mpisland.c"`, and `.asciz "mplib.c"` as evidence for file ownership.
Preserved build integration by replacing one object file with three ordered object files in `TEXT_O_FILES`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review summaries were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile `asm/melee/mp/mpcoll.s` into C.
Find later PRs that decompile `asm/melee/mp/mplib.s` into C.
Find later PRs that decompile `asm/melee/mp/mpisland.s` into C.
Search for changes to `obj_files.mk` around mp object ordering after PR 226.
Search for commits that rename `func_80041C78` or other early mpcoll functions.
